records.ety: [Pref. dis- + plant: cf. OF. desplanter, F. dplanter.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Displanted; p. pr. & vb. n. Displanting.]

defs: To remove (what is planted or fixed); to unsettle and take away; to displace; to root out; as, to displant inhabitants.
quotes: I did not think a look, Or a poor word or two, could have displanted Such a fixed constancy.

defs: To strip of what is planted or settled; as, to displant a country of inhabitants.
